title: Nephrodium gymnogrammoides
intro: Nephrodium gymnogrammoides, also known as Maidenhair fern, is a species of vascular plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium gymnogrammoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Maidenhair fern include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Nephrodium gymnogrammoides is a species of fern, specifically belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a part of the genus Nephrodium, which is now often considered a synonym of the genus Dryopteris. The species Nephrodium gymnogrammoides falls under the broader classification of the phylum Pteridophyta and the division Pteridophyta.
